title: phlomis oblongifolia
intro: Phlomis oblongifolia, also known as the Jerusalem sage, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its greyish-green leaves and pale yellow flowers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant is valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Phlomis oblongifolia include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in various Middle Eastern and Mediterranean dishes.
